sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Hellas Verona is in poor form, winless in their last 7 matches with only 2 goals scored in their last 5 games. Como, on the other hand, is in excellent shape with 6 consecutive wins, scoring consistently (9 goals in last 5 matches). This stark contrast in form heavily favors Como.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Como has the upper hand with a win (3-2) and a draw (1-1). They’ve scored in both encounters, while Verona struggled defensively, conceding in each game.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Verona ranks 15th with 31 goals scored (16th in league) and 64 conceded (19th), showing defensive frailties. Como, at 10th, has a balanced record with 48 goals scored and 49 conceded, demonstrating better attacking output. Verona’s low possession (38.9%) and Como’s late-game scoring trend (28.6% of goals after 76') could play a role.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Verona misses key midfielders like Ondrej Duda, impacting creativity. Como’s Patrick Cutrone (7 goals, 4 assists) and Gabriel Strefezza (in-form with 3 goals in last 5) are threats, though they also have notable absences like Assane Diao.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Verona’s high foul count (2nd most in league) and yellow cards (88, worst in league) could lead to disciplinary issues against Como’s attacking pace. Como’s ability to create big chances and score late may exploit Verona’s defensive lapses.
